task_completion.description: Open the 'Getting Started with WordPress' post in the WordPress editor
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress editor (block editor or classic editor) is open, displaying the title 'Getting Started with WordPress'.

task_completion.description: Add the 'Frequently Asked Questions' section with the three specified Q&A pairs
task_completion.visual_evidence: The post content includes a heading for 'Frequently Asked Questions' and the three exact questions and answers provided in the task description.

task_completion.description: Include valid JSON-LD Schema.org 'FAQPage' structured data within the post
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Custom HTML block or the code editor view shows a `<script type="application/ld+json">` tag containing the `FAQPage` schema that matches the three Q&A pairs.

task_completion.description: Create an internal link to the article '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A hyperlink is visible in the post content pointing to the '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs' article. The link URL should end with '10-essential-wordpress-plugins-every-site-needs'.

task_completion.description: Set the post excerpt to the specified text
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Excerpt' field in the right sidebar of the WordPress editor contains the exact text: 'A complete beginner's guide to getting started with WordPress, including features, benefits, and frequently asked questions.'

task_completion.description: Update the post to save all changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Update' button in the top right corner is clicked, or a 'Post updated' notification is visible on the screen.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress web interface to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the WordPress admin dashboard and post editor, rather than terminal commands or direct database modifications.

integrity.description: The JSON-LD schema is embedded as code, not plain text
integrity.visual_evidence: The schema is placed inside a Custom HTML block or added via the code editor, rather than being typed as visible plain text in a standard paragraph block.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a real web browser
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser UI elements (address bar, tabs) are visible, showing the local WordPress installation URL.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected excerpt is exactly: 'A complete beginner's guide to getting started with WordPress, including features, benefits, and frequently asked questions.' The expected questions are: 'Is WordPress free?', 'Do I need to know coding to use WordPress?', and 'What is the difference between WordPress.org and WordPress.com?'. The expected answers are: 'Yes, the core WordPress software is 100% free and open-source.', 'No, you can build a complete website without writing any code using themes and plugins.', and 'WordPress.org is the self-hosted software, while WordPress.com is a hosting service.'. The internal link should point to the post '10 Essential WordPress Plugins Every Site Needs', which has the slug '10-essential-wordpress-plugins-every-site-needs'.
